Commercial Mar 24, 2026

Here’s Why Airlines Keep Refurbishing Boeing 777-300ER Cabins Instead Of Replacing Them

Airlines refurbish their Boeing 777-300ER cabins as it offers a more cost-effective solution and improves the efficiency of an aircraft's lifecycle.

Cabin refurbishment extends 777-300ER profitability by 5-10 years, making mid-life upgrades more economical than fleet replacement during tight capital cycles.

See Simple Flying →

Space-based IoT connectivity advances could enable aircraft to access redundant communication networks beyond traditional terrestrial coverage, enhancing operational safety and data transmission capabilities.

Regulatory Mar 24, 2026

FAA Updates GNSS Interference Guide Months After First Release

Update adds pilot procedures and expands detail on recognizing and reporting jamming and spoofing.

FAA's revised interference guide now provides actionable pilot procedures for detecting and reporting GPS threats, addressing critical gaps that could improve real-time response to jamming incidents.

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS shifts aviation safety from investigating accidents after they occur to systematically identifying and mitigating hazards before incidents happen.

El Al cuts capacity by 95% as Israel limits Ben Gurion Traffic

Israel's severe airport capacity restrictions force El Al to operate near skeleton service levels, signaling potential systemic economic impact across the carrier's network and regional aviation competitiveness.

Effective SRM requires organizations to systematically implement controls and formally document acceptance of risks that cannot be eliminated, shifting from theory to operational compliance.

China's Astronstone is advancing reusable launch technology with innovative chopstick-style recovery mechanism, signaling intensifying competition in the commercial space sector.

Aerospace Mar 24, 2026

Space Development Agency slows satellite launches to focus on on-orbit performance

The agency’s acting director says checkout, orbit-raising and network testing are pacing the program The post Space Development Agency slows satellite launches to focus on on-orbit performance appeared first on SpaceNews.

Operational maturation of existing orbital assets now constrains the SDA's deployment cadence more than manufacturing capacity, signaling a shift from launch-focused to mission-capability development priorities.

Op-Ed: Repeated government shutdowns normalize aviation instability

U.S. Government shutdowns disrupt airport operations and a variety of private businesses, from airlines and bizjet operators to airport shops and restaurants. Please enjoy this Op-Ed contribution from Mohamed Yousuf, the CEO and founder of Smart Workforce AI. When government shutdowns occur, they...

Cyclical government shutdowns create compounding operational inefficiencies across aviation stakeholders, eroding competitive advantage and increasing systemic vulnerability to disruption.

Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use t

Eastern Iowa Airport's mixed-use model supporting everything from flight training to commercial air carrier operations demonstrates the critical infrastructure role regional airports play in distributed pilot workforce development.

Aerospace Mar 24, 2026

NASA halts work on Gateway to develop a lunar base

NASA is halting plans to develop the lunar Gateway and instead focusing on the development of a lunar base. The post NASA halts work on Gateway to develop a lunar base appeared first on SpaceNews.

NASA's shift from Gateway to surface-based lunar infrastructure reflects changing priorities for sustained Moon operations over orbital staging, impacting aerospace contractor competition.

Here's Why Airbus Doesn't Seem Fazed Over Boeing's Push For The 737 MAX 10

Airbus isn't losing sleep over Boeing's 737 MAX 10, and here's why.

Airbus's A321neo family already captures the high-capacity single-aisle market, making Boeing's delayed MAX 10 entry strategically less threatening than it might appear.

Military Mar 24, 2026

Space Force officer shares intelligence on threats to space systems

WASHINGTON – Electronic warfare is a growing threat to U.S. space systems, according to a March 23 unclassified briefing by U.S. Space Force Chief Master Sergeant Ron Lerch, senior enlisted advisor to the Deputy Chief of Space Operations for Intelligence. During the Satellite 2026 presentation, L...

Electronic warfare threats to space infrastructure require aviation industry collaboration on resilience protocols as satellite-dependent systems underpin modern air traffic management.

Surface Safety Spotlight – Los Angeles International Airport, CA (LAX) – From the Flight Deck

Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) is one of the nation’s busiest commercial airports. This short video highlights a runway incursion hazard where pilots exiting Runway 24R, particularly via taxi

Runway exit geometry at LAX's 24R creates systematic confusion for departing pilots, revealing how airport design flaws—not just pilot error—drive incursion incidents at major hubs.

Industry Mar 24, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across all three domains—airline, airport, and flight—offers a more immediate pathway to sustainability than waiting for technology breakthroughs alone.

A key element of this research involves observing reflected light from exoplanets, which can reveal indicators of Earth-like featur...

Spectroscopic analysis of exoplanet reflected light could enable detection of biosignatures, advancing the timeline for identifying potentially habitable worlds beyond our solar system.

Aerospace Mar 24, 2026

NASA’s Water-Hunting Tool Will Help Scout Moon’s South Pole

NASA is joining international partners to hunt for ice on the Moon in support of future human exploration. The agency is providing a water-detecting instrument, the Neutron Spectrometer System (NSS), to the Lunar Polar Exploration (LUPEX) mission led by JAXA (Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency) a...

NASA's neutron spectrometer on JAXA's LUPEX mission will provide critical ice-mapping data to validate lunar resource accessibility before committing to permanent human settlement infrastructure.
